title: Seeds, Replay, Settings, Save Data, and Migration · Python Build Room
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds versioned replay and safe-state schema from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned replay and safe-state schema while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record generator and solver versions, seed, dimensions, policies, assistance, action sequence, and deterministic state identity.
Replay logical commands and algorithm steps independently from wall-clock animation timing.
Validate version, types, bounds, enums, lengths, unknown fields, and cross-field invariants before constructing state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Replay Identity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 3609 during a corrupted-state recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop State Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-state-migration-1bfino" that uses case 5820 to validate replay identity, logical actions, bounded JSON state, atomic replacement, and version migration. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop State Migration.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns State Migration into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop State Migration, keep State Migration connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-state-migration-1bfino
# Competency: maze-state-migration.
# Case 5820: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_state_migration_1bfino(recorded_actions: int = 36, replayed_actions: int = 36, schema_version: int = 3, invalid_fields: int = 0, replay_digest_match: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bfino = "1bfino-onifb1-5820"
    assert evidence_variant_1bfino
    evidence_axes_1bfino = (
        "grid1bfino",
        "topology5820",
        "searchonifb1",
        "event1bfino5820",
        "access58201bfino",
        "recoveryonifb15820",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bfino)) == 6
    if recorded_actions < 0 or replayed_actions < 0:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-count"
    if replayed_actions != recorded_actions:
        return False, replayed_actions, "action-reconciliation"
    if schema_version < 1 or invalid_fields > 0:
        return False, invalid_fields, "schema-validation"
    if not replay_digest_match:
        return False, replayed_actions, "replay-divergence"
    assert replayed_actions == recorded_actions
    return True, replayed_actions, "versioned-replay-state"
